Definition (EN): The semantics of KerML specify how a KerML model is to be interpreted.
Context/Example (EN): The semantics of KerML specify how a KerML model is to be interpreted. The semantics are defined in terms of the abstract syntax representation of the model, and only for models which are valid relative to the structure and constraints specified for the KerML abstract syntax (see 8.3).

Definition (EN): These constraints specify relationships that are semantically required in a SysML model (see 8.4.1 ), particularly relationships with elements in the Kernel Semantic Library (see 9.2 ).
Context/Example (EN): These constraints may be violated by a model as entered by a user or as interchanged. In this case, a tool may satisfy the constraints by introducing implied relationships into the model, it may simply report their violation, or it may ignore the violations.

Definition (EN): A tool may insert certain implied Relationships into the model in order to meet the semantic constraints.
Context/Example (EN): For specialization constraints applying to Definitions, the implied Relationship is a Subclassification, as given in Table 31.

Definition (EN): These constraints require that Definition or Usage elements of a certain kind directly or indirectly specialize some specific base Definition or Usage from the Kernel Semantic Library or the Systems Model Library.
Context/Example (EN): Specialization constraints always have the word Specialization in their name. For example, checkPartDefinitionSpecialization requires that a PartDefinition directly or indirectly specialize the PartDefinition Parts::Part from the Systems Model Library.

Definition (EN): These constraints require that certain Usages in a model have Redefinition relationships with certain other Usages (or KerML Features) in the model.
Context/Example (EN): Redefinition constraints always have the word Redefinition in their name. For example, checkRenderingUsageRedefinition requires a Redefinition on a RenderingUsage used to specify a viewRendering.

Equivalent (JA): 型フィーチャリング制約
Definition (EN): These constraints require that certain Usages in a model have TypeFeaturing relationships with certain other Definitions or Usages in the model.
Context/Example (EN): The SysML specification includes only one constraint in this category, checkVariationUsageTypeFeaturing, which applies to a variant Usage in a variation model.

Equivalent (JA): バインディングコネクタ制約
Definition (EN): These constraints require that BindingConnectors exist between certain Features in a model.
Context/Example (EN): For a SysML model, it is allowable for binding-connector constraints to be satisfied by SysML BindingConnectorAsUsages, rather than plain KerML BindingConnectors.

Definition (EN): A SysML Definition has the semantics of a KerML Classifier (see [KerML, 8.4.3.3]).
Context/Example (EN): However, a Definition element is always either instantiated as one of its more specific subclasses (e.g., AttributeDefinition, ItemDefinition, etc.), or with at least one semantic metadata annotation.

Definition (EN): A SysML Usage has the semantics of a KerML Feature (see [KerML, 8.4.3.4]).
Context/Example (EN): However, a Usage element is always either instantiated as one of its more specific subclasses (e.g., AttributeUsage, ItemUsage, etc.), or with at least one semantic metadata annotation.

Equivalent (JA): バリエーション定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A Definition or Usage with isVariation = true has additional semantic restrictions.
Context/Example (EN): Thus, a variation Definition of the form variation part def P { variant part p1; variant part p2; ... } has, with implied Relationships included, the equivalent kernel semantics of // KerML class P specializes Parts::Part { member feature p1 : P subsets Parts::parts; member feature p2 : P subsets Parts::parts; ... }

Equivalent (JA): バリアント使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): All the ownedMembers of the Definition or Usage be variant Usages.
Context/Example (EN): Similarly, a Usage of the form variation part p { variant part p1; variant part p2; ... } has, with implied Relationships included, the equivalent kernel semantics of // KerML feature p subsets Parts::parts { member feature p1 subsets p; member feature p2 subsets p; ... }

Definition (EN): An AttributeDefinition is a kind of Definition and a kind of KerML DataType.
Context/Example (EN): The base AttributeDefinition Attributes::AttributeValue (see 9.2.2.2.1 ) is just an alias for the KerML DataType Base::DataValue (see [KerML, 9.2.2]).

Definition (EN): An AttributeUsage is a kind of Usage that is syntactically required to be defined only by DataTypes (including AttributeDefinitions).
Context/Example (EN): The base AttributeUsage Attributes::attributeValues (see 9.2.2.2.2 ) is an alias for the KerML Feature Base::dataValues (see [KerML, 9.2.2]).

Definition (EN): An EnumerationDefinition is a kind of AttributeDefinition all of whose instances are limited to specific set of enumerated values.
Context/Example (EN): However, an EnumerationDefinition is also required to have isVariation = true, and its enumeratedValues are then just its variants (see 8.4.2.3 on the semantics of variation Definitions).

Equivalent (JA): オカレンス定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An OccurrenceDefinition is a kind of Definition and a kind of KerML Class.
Context/Example (EN): The kernel checkClassSpecialization constraint requires that it specialize the base Class Occurrences::Occurrence from the Kernel Semantic Library (see [KerML, 9.2.4]).

Equivalent (JA): オカレンス使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An OccurrenceUsage is a kind of Usage that is syntactically required to be defined only by Classes (including OccurrenceDefinitions).
Context/Example (EN): checkOccurrenceUsageSpecialization requires that it specialize the Feature Occurrences::occurrences from the Kernel Model Library (see [KerML, 9.2.4]), which is typed by Occurrences::Occurrence.

Equivalent (JA): イベントオカレンス使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An EventOccurrenceUsage is a kind of OccurrenceUsage that is required to always be referential by the validateEventOccurrenceUsageIsReference.
Context/Example (EN): In addition, if an EventOccurrenceUsage is an ownedFeature of an OccurrenceDefinition or OccurrenceUsage, then the checkEventOccurrenceUsageSpecialization constraint requires that it specialize the kernel Feature Occurrences::Occurrence::timeEnclosedOccurrences (see [KerML, 9.2.4]).

Definition (EN): An ItemDefinition is a kind of OccurrenceDefinition and a kind of KerML Structure.
Context/Example (EN): The checkItemDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that an ItemDefinition specialize the base ItemDefinition Items::Item (see 9.2.3.2.1 ), which subclassifies the kernel Class Objects::Object (see [KerML, 9.2.5]).

Definition (EN): An ItemUsage is a kind of OccurrenceUsage.
Context/Example (EN): checkItemUsageSpecialization requires that an ItemUsage specialize the base ItemUsage Items::items (see 9.2.3.2.2 ), which subsets the Kernel Feature Objects::objects (see [KerML, 9.2.5]).

Definition (EN): A PartDefinition is a kind of ItemDefinition.
Context/Example (EN): The checkPartDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a PartDefinition specialize the base PartDefinition Parts::Part (see 9.2.4.2.1 ), which subclassifies Items::Item (see 9.2.3.2.1 ).

Definition (EN): A PartUsage is a kind of ItemUsage.
Context/Example (EN): checkPartUsageSpecialization requires that a PartUsage specialize the base PartUsage Parts::parts (see 9.2.4.2.2 ).

Definition (EN): A PortDefinition is a kind of OccurrenceDefinition and a kind of KerML Structure.
Context/Example (EN): The checkPortDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a PortDefinition specialize the base PortDefinition Ports::Port (see 9.2.5.2.1 ), which subclassifies the kernel Class Objects::Object (see [KerML, 9.2.5]).

Definition (EN): A PortUsage is a kind of OccurrenceUsage that is syntactically restricted to be defined only by PortDefinitions.
Context/Example (EN): checkPortUsageSpecialization requires that a PortUsage specialize the base PortUsage Ports::ports (see 9.2.5.2.2 ), which subsets the kernel Feature Objects::objects (see [KerML, 9.2.5]).

Definition (EN): A ConnectionDefinition is a kind of PartDefinition and a kind of KerML AssociationStructure.
Context/Example (EN): The checkConnectionDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a ConnectionDefinition specialize the base ConnectionDefinition Connections::Connection (see 9.2.6.2.3 ), which subclassifies the PartDefinition Parts::Part (see 9.2.4.2.1 ) and the kernel AssociationStructure Objects::LinkObject (see [KerML, 9.2.5]).

Definition (EN): A ConnectionUsage is a PartUsage and a ConnectorAsUsage, which is a kind of KerML Connector.
Context/Example (EN): The checkConnectionUsageSpecialization constraint requires that a ConnectionUsage specialize the base ConnectionUsage Connections::connections (see 9.2.6.2.4 ), which is defined by Connections::Connection and subsets the PartUsage Parts::parts (see 9.2.4.2.2 ) and the kernel Feature Objects::linkObjects (see [KerML, 9.2.5]).

Equivalent (JA): バインディングコネクタ使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A BindingConnectorAsUsage is a kind of ConnectorAsUsage and a kind of KerML BindingConnector.
Context/Example (EN): The checkBindingConnectorSpecialization constraint requires that BindingConnectorAsUsages specialize the kernel Feature Links:selfLink, which is typed by the Association SelfLink (see [KerML, 9.2.3]).

Definition (EN): A SuccessionAsUsage is a kind of FlowUsage and a kind of KerML Succession.
Context/Example (EN): The checkSuccessionSpecialization constraint requires that a SuccessionAsUsage specialize the KerML Feature Occurrences::happensBeforeLinks (see [KerML, 9.2.4]), which is typed by the Association HappensBefore.

Equivalent (JA): インターフェース定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An InterfaceDefinition is a kind of ConnectionDefinition whose connectionEnds are syntactically restricted to to be PortUsages.
Context/Example (EN): The checkInterfaceDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that an InterfaceDefinition specialize the base InterfaceDefinition Interfaces::Interface (see 9.2.7.2.3 ), which subclassifies the ConnectionDefinition Connection::Connection (see 9.2.6.2.3 ).

Equivalent (JA): インターフェース使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An InterfaceUsage is a kind of ConnectionUsage whose connectionDefinitions are syntactically restricted to to be only InterfaceDefinitions.
Context/Example (EN): The checkInterfaceUsageSpecialization constraint requires that an InterfaceUsage specialize the base InterfaceUsage Interfaces::interfaces (see 9.2.7.2.4 ), which is defined by the InterfaceDefinition Interfaces::Interface (see 9.2.7.2.3 ) and subsets the ConnectionUsage Connection::connections (see 9.2.6.2.4 ).

Definition (EN): An AllocationDefinition is a ConnectionDefinition that specifies that some or all of the responsibility to realize the intent of the source is allocated to the target instances.
Context/Example (EN): The checkAllocationDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that an AllocationDefinition specialize the base AllocationDefinition Allocations::Allocation (see 9.2.8.2.1 ), which subclassifies the ConnectionDefinition Connection::BinaryConnection (see 9.2.6.2.1 ).

Definition (EN): An AllocationUsage is a kind of binary ConnectionUsage whose connectionDefinitions are syntactically restricted to to be only AllocationDefinitions.
Context/Example (EN): The checkAllocationUsageSpecialization constraint requires that an AllocationUsage specialize the base AllocationUsage Allocations::allocations (see 9.2.8.2.2 ), which is defined by the AllocationDefinition Allocations::Allocation (see 9.2.8.2.1 ) and subsets the ConnectionUsage Connection::binaryConnections (see 9.2.6.2.4 ) (see 9.2.6.2.2 ).

Definition (EN): A FlowDefinition is a kind of ActionDefinition, and a kind of KerML Interaction.
Context/Example (EN): The checkFlowDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a FlowDefinition specialize the base FlowDefinition Flows::MessageAction (see 9.2.9.2.4 ), which subclassifies the ActionDefinition Actions::Action (see 9.2.10.2.4 ), as well as the kernel Association Links::Link (see [KerML, 9.2.3]).

Definition (EN): A FlowUsage is a kind of ActionUsage and a kind of KerML Flow that is syntactically restricted to be defined by only KerML Interactions (including FlowDefinitions).
Context/Example (EN): The checkFlowUsageSpecification constraint requires that a FlowUsage specialize the base FlowUsage Flows::messages (see 9.2.9.2.5 ), which is typed by the FlowDefinition Flows::Message (see 9.2.9.2.3 ) and subsets the ActionUsage Actions::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ), and the kernel Step Transfers::transfers (see [KerML, 9.2.7]).

Equivalent (JA): 継承フロー使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A SuccessionFlowUsage is a kind of FlowUsage and a kind of KerML SuccessionFlow.
Context/Example (EN): The checkSuccessionFlowUsageSpecialization constraint requires that it specialize the FlowUsage Flows::successionFlows (see 9.2.9.2.7 ), which is defined by the FlowConnectionDefinition SuccessionFlowConnection (see 9.2.9.2.6 ; see also 8.4.12.1 ) and subsets the FlowUsage Flows::flows (see 9.2.9.2.2 ) and the kernel Step flowTransfersBefore (see [KerML, 9.2.7]).

Equivalent (JA): アクション定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An ActionDefinition is a kind of OccurrenceDefinition and a kind of KerML Behavior.
Context/Example (EN): The checkActionDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that an ActionDefinition specialize the base ActionDefinition Actions::Action (see 9.2.10.2.4 ), which subclassifies the kernel Behavior Performances::Performance (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Equivalent (JA): アクション使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An ActionUsage is a kind of OccurrenceUsage and a kind of KerML Step.
Context/Example (EN): The checkActionUsageSpecialization requires that an ActionUsage specialize the base ActionUsage Actions::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::Action (see 9.2.10.2.4 ) and subsets the kernel Step Performances::performances (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Definition (EN): A Succession in the body of an ActionDefinition or ActionUsage may be syntactically declared with a guard Expression. In this case, it is actually parsed as a TransitionUsage, with the guard Expression and the declared Succession nested in the TransitionUsage (see 8.2.2.17.8 ).
Context/Example (EN): The checkTransitionUsageActionSpecialization constraint requires that a composite TransitionUsage whose ownedType is an ActionDefinition or ActionUsage, but not a StateDefinition or StateUsage, specialize the ActionUsage Actions::Action::decisionTransitions (see 9.2.10.2.4 ), which is typed by the ActionDefinition Actions::DecisionTransitionAction (see 9.2.10.2.10 ).

Definition (EN): A ControlNode is a kind of ActionUsage that has no inherent behavior but is used to control other Actions within a containing Action.
Context/Example (EN): The checkControlNodeSpecialization requires that a ControlNode specialize the ActionUsage Actions::Action::controls (see 9.2.10.2.4 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::ControlAction (see 9.2.10.2.8 ) and subsets the composite ActionUsage Actions::Action::subactions (further implying that a ControlNode must be composite).

Equivalent (JA): 送信アクション使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A SendActionUsage is a kind of ActionUsage that specifies the sending of a payload given by the result of its payloadArgument Expression via a MessageTransfer whose source is given by the result of the senderArgument Expression and whose target is given by the receiverArgument Expression.
Context/Example (EN): The checkSendActionUsageSpecialization requires that a SendActionUsage specialize the ActionUsage Actions::sendActions (see 9.2.10.2.23 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::SendAction (see 9.2.10.2.22 ) and subsets the ActionUsage Actions::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ) and the kernel Feature Transfers::sendPerformances (see [KerML, 9.2.7]).

Equivalent (JA): 受信アクション使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An AcceptActionUsage is a kind of ActionUsage that specifies the acceptance of an incomingTransfer from the Occurrence given by the result of its receiverArgument Expression.
Context/Example (EN): The checkAcceptActionUsageSpecialization requires that (unless it is the triggerAction of a TransitionUsage, see 8.4.14.3 ) an AcceptActionUsage specialize the ActionUsage Actions::acceptActions (see 9.2.10.2.2 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::AcceptAction (see 9.2.10.2.1 ) and subsets Actions::Action::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ) and the kernel Feature Transfers::acceptPerformances (see [KerML, 9.2.7]).

Equivalent (JA): 割り当てアクション使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An AssignmentActionUsage is an ActionUsage that is defined, directly or indirectly, by the ActionDefinition AssignmentAction from the Systems Model Library.
Context/Example (EN): The checkAssignmentActionUsageSpecialization requires that an AssignmentActionUsage specialize the ActionUsage Actions::assignmentActions (see 9.2.10.2.7 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::AssignmentAction (see 9.2.10.2.6 ) and subsets Actions::Action::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ).

Equivalent (JA): 終了アクション使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A TerminateActionUsage is an ActionUsage that directly or indirectly specializes the ActionDefinition TerminateAction from the Systems Model Library, which causes a given terminatedOccurrence to end during its performance.
Context/Example (EN): The checkTerminateActionUsageSpecialization requires that a TerminateActionUsage specialize the ActionUsage Actions::terminateActions (see 9.2.10.2.25 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::TerminateAction (see 9.2.10.2.24 ) and subsets Actions::Action::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ).

Equivalent (JA): Ifアクション使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An IfActionUsage is a kind of ActionUsage that specifies that the thenAction ActionUsage should be performed if the result of the ifArgument Expression is true.
Context/Example (EN): The checkIfActionUsageSpecialization requires that If an IfActionUsage does not have an elseAction, it specialize the ActionUsage Actions::ifThenActions (see 9.2.10.2.15 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::IfThenAction (see 9.2.10.2.14 ) and subsets Actions::Action::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ).

Equivalent (JA): ループアクション使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A LoopActionUsage is a kind of ActionUsage that specifies that its bodyAction ActionUsage should be performed repeatedly.
Context/Example (EN): The checkWhileLoopActionUsageSpecialization requires that a WhileLoopActionUsage specialize the ActionUsage Actions::whileLoopActions (see 9.2.10.2.29 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::WhileLoopAction (see 9.2.10.2.28 ) and (indirectly) subsets Actions::Action::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ).

Equivalent (JA): 実行アクション使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A PerformActionUsage is a kind of ActionUsage and a kind of EventOccurrenceUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkPerformActionUsageSpecialization constraint requires that if a PerformActionUsage is an ownedFeature of a PartDefinition or PartUsage, then it must directly or indirectly specialize the ActionUsage Parts::Part::performedActions.

Definition (EN): A StateDefinition is a kind of ActionDefinition.
Context/Example (EN): The checkStateDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a StateDefinition specialize the base StateDefinition States::StateAction (see 9.2.11.2.1 ), which specializes the ActionDefinition Actions::Action (see 9.2.10.2.4 ) and the kernel Behavior StatePerformances::StatePerformance (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Definition (EN): A StateUsage is a kind of ActionUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkStateUsageSpecialization requires that a StateUsage specialize the base StateUsage States::stateActions (see 9.2.11.2.2 ), which is defined by the StateDefinition States::StateAction (see 9.2.11.2.1 ) and subsets the ActionUsage Actions::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ).

Definition (EN): A TransitionUsage is a kind of ActionUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkTransitionUsageSpecialization requires that a TransitionUsage specialize the ActionUsage Actions::transitionActions (see 9.2.10.2.27 ), which is defined by the ActionDefinition Actions::TransitionAction (see 9.2.10.2.26 ) and subsets the ActionUsage Actions::actions (see 9.2.10.2.5 ).

Definition (EN): An ExhibitStateUsage is a kind of StateUsage and a kind of PerformActionUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkExhibitStateUsageSpecialization constraint requires that if an ExhibitStateUsage is an ownedFeature of a PartDefinition or PartUsage, then it must directly or indirectly specialize the StateUsage Parts::Part::exhibitStates (see 9.2.4.2.1 ), which subsets State::states (see 7.18 ) and Parts::Part::performedActions.

Definition (EN): A CalculationDefinition is a kind of ActionDefinition and a kind of KerML Function.
Context/Example (EN): The checkCalculationDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a CalculationDefinition specialize the base CalculationDefinition Calculations::Calculation (see 9.2.12.2.1 ), which subclassifies the ActionDefinition Actions::Action (see 9.2.10.2.4 ) and the kernel Behavior Performances::Evaluation (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Definition (EN): A CalculationUsage is a kind of ActionUsage and a kind of KerML Expression.
Context/Example (EN): The checkCalculationUsageSpecialization requires that a CalculationUsage specialize the base CalculationUsage Calculations::calculations (see 9.2.12.2.2 ), which is defined by the CalculationDefinition Calculations::Calculation (see 9.2.12.2.1 ) and subsets the ActionUsage Actions::actions (see 9.2.10.2.4 ) and the kernel Step Performances::evaluations (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Definition (EN): A ConstraintDefinition is a kind of OccurrenceDefinition and a kind of KerML Predicate.
Context/Example (EN): The checkConstraintDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a ConstraintDefinition specialize the base ConstraintDefinition Constraints::ConstraintCheck (see 9.2.10.2.4 ), which subclassifies the kernel Predicate Performances::BooleanEvaluation (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Definition (EN): A ConstraintUsage is a kind of OccurrenceUsage and a kind of KerML Expression.
Context/Example (EN): The checkConstraintUsageSpecialization requires that a ConstraintUsage specialize the base ConstraintUsage Constraints::constraintChecks (see 9.2.13.2.3 ), which is defined by the ConstraintDefinition Constraints::ConstraintCheck (see 9.2.13.2.2 ) and subsets the kernel Step Performances::booleanEvaluations (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Equivalent (JA): アサート制約使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An AssertConstraintUsage is a kind of ConstraintUsage and a kind of KerML Invariant.
Context/Example (EN): The checkAssertConstraintUsageSpecialization constraint requires that an AssertConstraintUsage specialize one of the following: If the AssertConstraintUsage is not negated, ConstraintUsage Constraints::assertedConstraintChecks (see 9.2.13.2.1 ), which subsets the ConstraintUsage Constraints::constraintChecks (see 9.2.13.2.3 ) and the kernel Expression Performances::trueEvaluations (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Definition (EN): A RequirementDefinition is a kind of ConstraintDefinition.
Context/Example (EN): The checkRequirementDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a RequirementDefinition specialize the base RequirementDefinition Requirements::RequirementCheck (see 9.2.14.2.8 ), which specializes the ConstraintDefinition Constraints::ConstraintCheck (see 9.2.13.2.2 ).

Definition (EN): A RequirementUsage is a kind of ConstraintUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkRequirementUsageSpecialization requires that a RequirementUsage specialize the base RequirementUsage Requirements::requirementChecks (see 9.2.14.2.9 ), which subsets Constraints::constraintChecks (see 9.2.13.2.3 ).

Definition (EN): A SatisfyRequirementUsage is a kind of RequirementUsage and a kind of AssertConstraintUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkSatisfyRequirementUsageBindingConnector constraint requires that a SatisfyRequirementUsage have a BindingConnector between its subjectParameter and some other Feature, which is then considered to be the satisfyingFeature of the SatisfyRequirementUsage.

Definition (EN): A ConcernDefinition is a kind of RequirementDefinition.
Context/Example (EN): The checkConcernDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a ConcernDefinition specialize the base ConcernDefinition Requirements::ConcernCheck (see 9.2.14.2.1 ), which specializes the RequirementDefinition Requirements::RequirementCheck (see 9.2.14.2.8 ).

Definition (EN): A ConcernUsage is a kind of RequirementUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkConcernUsageSpecialization requires that a ConcernUsage specialize the base ConcernUsage Requirements::concernChecks (see 9.2.14.2.2 ), which subsets Requirements::requirementChecks (see 9.2.14.2.9 ).

Definition (EN): A CaseDefinition is a kind of CalculationDefinition.
Context/Example (EN): The checkCaseDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a CaseDefinition specialize the base CaseDefinition Cases::Case (see 9.2.15.2.1 ), which subclassifies the CalculationDefinition Calculations::Calculation (see 9.2.12.2.1 ).

Definition (EN): A CaseUsage is a kind of CalculationUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkCaseUsageSpecialization requires that a CaseUsage specialize the base CaseUsage Cases::cases (see 9.2.15.2.2 ), which is defined by the CaseDefinition Cases::Case (see 9.2.15.2.1 ) and subsets the CalculationUsage Calculations::calculations (see 9.2.10.2.4 ).

Equivalent (JA): 分析ケース定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An AnalysisCaseDefinition is a kind of CaseDefinition.
Context/Example (EN): The checkAnalysisCaseDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that an AnalysisCaseDefinition specialize the base AnalysisCaseDefinition AnalysisCases::AnalysisCase (see 9.2.16.2.1 ), which subclassifies the CaseDefinition Cases::Case (see 9.2.15.2.1 ).

Equivalent (JA): 分析ケース使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An AnalysisCaseUsage is a kind of CaseUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkAnalysisCaseUsageSpecialization constraint requires that an AnalysisCaseUsage specialize the base AnalysisCaseUsage AnalysisCases::analysisCases (see 9.2.16.2.2 ), which is defined by the AnalysisCaseDefinition AnalysisCases::AnalysisCase (see 9.2.16.2.1 ) and subsets the CaseUsage Cases::cases (see 9.2.15.2.2 ).

Equivalent (JA): 検証ケース定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A VerificationCaseDefinition is a kind of CaseDefinition.
Context/Example (EN): The checkVerificationCaseDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a VerificationCaseDefinition specialize the base VerificationCaseDefinition VerificationCases::VerificationCase (see 9.2.17.2.3 ), which subclassifies the CaseDefinition Cases::Case (see 9.2.15.2.1 ).

Equivalent (JA): 検証ケース使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A VerificationCaseUsage is a kind of CaseUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkVerificationCaseUsageSpecialization constraint requires that a VerificationCaseUsage specialize the base VerificationCaseUsage VerificationCases::verificationCases (see 9.2.17.2.4 ), which is defined by the VerificationCaseDefinition VerificationCases::VerificationCase (see 9.2.17.2.3 ) and subsets the CaseUsage Cases::cases (see 9.2.15.2.2 ).

Equivalent (JA): ユースケース定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A UseCaseDefinition is a kind of CaseDefinition.
Context/Example (EN): The checkUseCaseDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a UseCaseDefinition specialize the base UseCaseDefinition UseCases::UseCase (see 9.2.18.2.1 ), which subclassifies the CaseDefinition Cases::Case (see 9.2.15.2.1 ).

Equivalent (JA): ユースケース使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A UseCaseUsage is a kind of CaseUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkUseCaseUsageSpecialization constraint requires that a UseCaseUsage specialize the base UseCaseUsage UseCases::useCases (see 9.2.18.2.2 ), which is defined by the UseCaseDefinition UseCases::UseCase (see 9.2.18.2.1 ) and subsets the CaseUsage Cases::cases (see 9.2.15.2.2 ).

Equivalent (JA): インクルードユースケース使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): An IncludeUseCaseUsage is a kind of UseCaseUsage and a kind of PerformActionUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkUseCaseUsageSpecialization constraint requires that if an IncludeUseCaseUsage has an owningType that is a UseCaseDefinition or UseCaseUsage, then it specialize the UseCaseUsage UseCases::UseCase::includedUseCases (see 9.2.18.2.1 ), which subsets UseCases::useCases (see 9.2.18.2.2 ) and the kernel Feature Performances::Performance::enclosedPerformances (see [KerML, 9.2.6]).

Definition (EN): A ViewDefinition is a kind of PartDefinition that specifies how a view artifact is constructed to satisfy a viewpoint.
Context/Example (EN): The checkViewDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a ViewDefinition specialize the base ViewDefinition Views::View (see 9.2.19.2.10 ), which subclassifies Parts::Part (see 9.2.4.2.1 ).

Definition (EN): A ViewUsage is a kind of PartUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkViewUsageSpecialization constraint requires that a ViewUsage specialize the base ViewUsage Views::views (see 9.2.19.2.14 ).

Equivalent (JA): ビューポイント定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A ViewpointDefinition is a kind of RequirementDefinition that specifies one or more stakeholder concerns that are to be satisfied by creating a view of a model.
Context/Example (EN): The checkViewpointDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a ViewpointDefinition specialize the base ViewpointDefinition Views::ViewpointCheck (see 9.2.19.2.11 ), which specializes the RequirementDefinition Requirements::RequirementCheck (see 9.2.14.2.8 ).

Equivalent (JA): ビューポイント使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A ViewpointUsage is a kind of RequirementUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkViewpointUsageSpecialization requires that a ViewpointUsage specialize the base ViewpointUsage Views::viewpoints (see 9.2.19.2.12 ), which subsets Requirements::requirementChecks (see 9.2.14.2.9 ).

Equivalent (JA): レンダリング定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A RenderingDefinition is a kind of PartDefinition that specifies how a view artifact is to be rendered.
Context/Example (EN): The checkRenderingDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a RenderingDefinition specialize the base RenderingDefinition Views::Rendering (see 9.2.19.2.6 ), which subclassifies Parts::Part (see 9.2.4.2.1 ).

Equivalent (JA): レンダリング使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A RenderingUsage is a kind of PartUsage.
Context/Example (EN): The checkRenderingUsageSpecialization constraint requires that a RenderingUsage specialize the base RenderingUsage Views::rendering (see 9.2.19.2.7 ).

Equivalent (JA): メタデータ定義 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A MetadataDefinition is a kind of ItemDefinition and a kind of KerML Metaclass.
Context/Example (EN): The checkMetadataDefinitionSpecialization constraint requires that a MetadataDefinition specialize the base MetadataDefinition Metadata::MetadataItem (see 9.2.21.2.1 ), which subclassifies Items::Item (see 9.2.3.2.1 ) and the kernel Metaclass Metaobjects::Metaobject (see [KerML, 9.2.16]).

Equivalent (JA): メタデータ使用 (意味論)
Definition (EN): A MetadataUsage is a kind of ItemUsage and a kind of KerML MetadataFeature.
Context/Example (EN): The checkMetadataUsageSpecialization constraint requires that a MetadataUsage specialize the base MetadataUsage Metadata::metadataItems (see 9.2.21.2.2 ), which is defined by Metadata::MetadataItem (see 9.2.21.2.1 ) and subsets Items::items (see 9.2.3.2.2 ) and the kernel Feature Metaobjects::metaobjects (see [KerML, 9.2.16]).
